title: narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. longispathus
intro: Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. longispathus, commonly known as the wild daffodil or long-spurred narcissus, is a subspecies of perennial flowering plant native to Europe, particularly in the UK. It possesses medicinal properties, primarily due to its alkaloids, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and as a diuretic. The plant offers several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Narcissus pseudonarcissus subsp. longispathus include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
